channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: US agencies should use advanced technology to identify mysterious drones, Schumer says
item.description: After weeks of fear and bewilderment about the drones buzzing over parts of New York and New Jersey, U.S. Senator Chuck Schumer is urging the federal government to deploy better drone-tracking technology to identify and ultimately stop the airborne pests.
item.dc:creator: BIANCA VÁZQUEZ TONESS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Point guard Dennis Schroder traded by Brooklyn to Golden State
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — Golden State has landed point guard Dennis Schroder in a trade with the Brooklyn Nets in exchange for injured guard De'Anthony Melton and guard Reece Beekman, the teams announced Sunday. The trade was agreed to on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: What we know about drones over New Jersey, other parts of the US
item.description: The drones have prompted concern from local residents, anger from lawmakers and endless speculation on social media.
item.dc:creator: Sean Noone, KRON4

item.title: Fewer jobs require a college degree: Indeed
item.description: The share of job postings requiring at least a bachelor's degree fell below 18% in October from roughly 20% before the pandemic, according to Indeed's 2025 U.S. Jobs and Hiring Trends Report.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: The original lyrics of 'Have Yourself a Merry Little Christmas' were extremely depressing
item.description: “Have Yourself a Merry Little Christmas” is already more melancholy than most classic Christmas songs, but its earlier version was a downright downer.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Photos: US storms bring heavy snow, dangerous ice and a tornado in California
item.description: In San Francisco, authorities issued the first-ever tornado warning. Meanwhile, upstate New York saw heavy snow, the Midwest encountered an ice storm, and severe weather warnings were issued around Lake Tahoe.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Storms across US bring heavy snow, dangerous ice and a tornado in California
item.description: OMAHA, Neb. (AP) — A tornado near a mall in central California swept up cars, uprooted trees and sent several people to the hospital. In San Francisco, authorities issued the first-ever tornado warning. Elsewhere, inclement weather plagued areas of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Millionaire boomers less likely to share their wealth: Charles Schwab
item.description: Data revealed that baby boomers, born from 1946 to 1964, plan to spend most of their wealth rather than reserve it for future generations.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Shooting at pop-up party in Houston leaves 2 teenagers dead, others wounded: police
item.description: A shooting in Houston, Texas, left a pair of teenagers dead and three others wounded after a pop-up party at a makeshift club Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Colorado two-way star Travis Hunter wins Heisman Trophy as college football's top player
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Travis Hunter turned every play into prime time — on both sides of the ball — and ultimately took home the Heisman Trophy. Now he’s got a leg up on his celebrity coach at Colorado.
item.dc:creator: MIKE FITZPATRICK, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Storms encase Iowa and eastern Nebraska in ice and generate first tornado warning in San Francisco
item.description: OMAHA, Neb. (AP) — A major ice storm created treacherous driving conditions across Iowa and eastern Nebraska this weekend and prompted temporary closures of Interstate 80 after numerous cars and trucks slid off the road.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: ABC settles Trump’s defamation suit for $15M
item.description: The settlement enables both the anchor and Trump to avoid sitting for court-ordered depositions next week.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: South Korea’s parliament votes to impeach President Yoon Suk Yeol over his martial law order
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea’s parliament on Saturday impeached President Yoon Suk Yeol over his stunning and short-lived martial law decree, a move that ended days of political paralysis but set up an intense debate over Yoon's fate, as jubilant crowds roared to celebrate another defiant m
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: VIDEO: San Francisco cafe employee takes down would-be thief
item.description: The video (above) shows a man wearing a black hoodie and mask walking into the cafe and trying to take an item from a customer dining inside.
item.dc:creator: Phil Mayer, WGN 9

item.title: Jamie Foxx reportedly injured in altercation at Beverly Hills restaurant
item.description: Foxx, who turned 57 on Friday, was dining with family at Mr. Chow when patrons at a nearby table hurled rude and vulgar insults to the "In Living Color" and "Ray" star, a witness told TMZ.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KRON4

item.title: 6 ways to avoid a financial holiday hangover
item.description: The festivity of December is replaced all too quickly by the due dates of January, when the bills from holiday spending and travel arrive.
item.dc:creator: Kimberly Palmer, KTLA 5

item.title: A look back at the most notable bankruptcies of 2024
item.description: This year saw its fair share of bankruptcies, with household names and century-old companies in the mix.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, ABC 4

item.title: Thousands of Santas and a few Grinches hit the streets for annual SantaCon bar crawls
item.description: Thousands of Kris Kringles and jolly Old St. Nicks are jamming streets, sidewalks and bars from Adelaide to Vancouver on Saturday for annual SantaCon charity pub crawls.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Pelosi 'on the mend' after hip replacement surgery in Germany
item.description: Former House Speaker Nancy Pelosi (D-Calif.), who was hospitalized during a trip to Luxembourg, underwent hip surgery early Saturday, according to a spokesperson.
item.dc:creator: Steff Danielle Thomas, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Detroit area's many Syrians are celebrating Assad's overthrow and planning long-delayed visits
item.description: Like millions of Syrians around the world, the thousands who have settled in the Detroit area are excited about the prospect of visiting now that rebels have overthrown the government of ousted President Bashar Assad.
item.dc:creator: Corey Williams And Mike Householder, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Lawmakers tell Apple, Google to prepare for TikTok ban
item.description: TikTok now plans to appeal to the Supreme Court and has asked the D.C. Circuit to put the law on hold while it appeals the case.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, Fox 5

item.title: Taylor Swift visits patients at Children's Mercy Hospital in Kansas City
item.description: Fresh off her final Era's Tour stop Sunday in Canada, Taylor Swift made a surprise visit at Children's Mercy Hospital.
item.dc:creator: Dave D'Marko, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Two states sue Glock over add-on allowing machine gun-like firing
item.description: Officials in New Jersey and Minnesota filed a lawsuit against gun manufacturer Glock Thursday, demanding the company suspend its sale of weapons that can be easily converted with a switch to allow over 1,000 rounds of rapid fire.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KRON4

item.title: Duke lacrosse players' rape accuser admits she lied
item.description: The former exotic dancer confessed to lying about the encounter in 2006 during her appearance on the “Let’s Talk with Kat” podcast.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Nearly half of US teens are online 'constantly': Pew report
item.description: Nearly half of American teenagers say they are online “constantly” despite concerns about the effects of social media and smartphones on their mental health, according to a new report published Thursday by the Pew Research Center.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Texas sues DuPont, 3M over 'forever chemicals' in consumer products
item.description: Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton (R) is suing companies that have historically made toxic “forever chemicals,” commonly referred to as PFAS, or products that contain them, alleging false advertising over their safety.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, ABC 4

item.title: Back trouble and brain fog bothered suspect in UnitedHealthcare CEO killing, his posts show
item.description: After Luigi Mangione made the difficult decision to undergo spinal surgery last year for chronic back pain, he became a proponent of the procedure that changed his life for the better.
item.dc:creator: LEA SKENE and JENNIFER SINCO KELLEHER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: McDonald's employee who called 911 in CEO's shooting is eligible for a reward, but it will take time
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — More than 400 tips were called into the New York Police Department's Crime Stoppers tip line during the five-day search for a masked gunman who ambushed and fatally shot UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson last week.
item.dc:creator: CLAUDIA LAUER, Associated Press, ABC 4
